description: Personalization v1.1 Enhancements — Add preferred name collection, quit attempts logarithmic scale, and custom triggers to the onboarding intake flow

userStories.title: Create database migration for v1.1 schema changes
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a migration file that adds preferred_name column, converts previous_attempts to TEXT, migrates existing data, and adds a CHECK constraint, so that the schema supports all v1.1 features.
acceptanceCriteria: Create migration file at supabase/migrations/20260215_personalization_v11.sql
Add preferred_name VARCHAR(50) nullable column to user_intake table
Alter previous_attempts from INTEGER to TEXT
Include data migration: '0'->'never', '1'->'once', '2'->'a_few', '3'->'many' (only for numeric strings)
Add CHECK constraint check_previous_attempts allowing NULL or one of: 'never', 'once', 'a_few', 'many', 'countless'
Include documented rollback SQL as comments at the bottom of the migration file
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: See spec v1.1: Schema Changes section for exact SQL. CHECK constraint must allow NULL (PostgreSQL passes NULL by default). Rollback SQL: drop constraint, reverse mapping, alter back to INTEGER, drop preferred_name.

userStories.title: Update TypeScript types for v1.1 fields
userStories.description: As a developer, I want IntakeData, IntakeResponse, and IntakeBody types updated for v1.1 schema changes, so that TypeScript catches type mismatches.
acceptanceCriteria: In composables/useIntake.ts: IntakeData has preferredName?: string field
In composables/useIntake.ts: IntakeData.previousAttempts type is string | undefined (was number | undefined)
In composables/useIntake.ts: IntakeResponse has preferred_name?: string field (snake_case)
In composables/useIntake.ts: IntakeResponse.previous_attempts type is string | undefined (was number | undefined)
In server/api/intake/index.post.ts: IntakeBody has preferredName?: string field
In server/api/intake/index.post.ts: IntakeBody.previousAttempts type is string | undefined (was number | undefined)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Clean type break — no union types for backwards compat. See spec v1.1: State Management Design for exact interfaces.

userStories.title: Update POST /api/intake for v1.1 fields
userStories.description: As a user completing onboarding, I want the intake API to accept my preferred name, string-based quit attempts, and custom triggers, so that my personalization data is stored correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: preferredName is trimmed, stored as NULL if empty/whitespace-only, rejected with 400 if over 50 chars
previousAttempts validated against allowed set: 'never', 'once', 'a_few', 'many', 'countless' — rejects with 400 if invalid
previousAttempts as integer (e.g. 2) returns 400 error (no backwards compat)
previousAttempts omitted stores NULL (not 0)
triggers with custom: prefixed entries are stored as-is
triggers with empty custom: prefix (e.g. 'custom:') returns 400 error
preferred_name is included in the upsert object to user_intake
previous_attempts uses normalized value (null if omitted) instead of || 0 default
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File: server/api/intake/index.post.ts. Change previous_attempts: body.previousAttempts || 0 to previous_attempts: normalizedPreviousAttempts. Add validation for preferredName length, previousAttempts allowed values, and custom: trigger entries.

userStories.title: Create prompt injection sanitization utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a utility function that sanitizes user-provided text before prompt injection, so that names and triggers cannot be used for prompt injection attacks.
acceptanceCriteria: New file created at server/utils/personalization/sanitize-user-text.ts
Exports sanitizeForPrompt(text: string): string function
Strips LLM role markers (SYSTEM:, ASSISTANT:, Human:, AI:, User:) at start of lines, case-insensitive
Strips markdown headers (lines starting with # through ######)
Strips triple backticks
Strips XML/HTML-like tags
Preserves normal names like 'Kevin', 'Skip', 'Ignore' without false positives
Trims leading/trailing whitespace
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: See spec v1.1: Prompt Injection Sanitization section for exact regex patterns. This utility is used by the context builder (NFR-4.3).

userStories.title: Update context builder for v1.1 fields
userStories.description: As the AI coach, I want the context builder to include preferred name, natural-language quit history, and formatted custom triggers, so that responses feel more personal.
acceptanceCriteria: IntakeUserContext interface in context-builder.ts adds preferredName: string | null, changes previousAttempts to string | null
getUserIntake() selects preferred_name from user_intake
formatContextForPrompt() includes USER: line with sanitized preferred_name wrapped in triple-backtick delimiters, omitted when null/empty
previousAttempts mapped via QUIT_ATTEMPTS_CONTEXT: never->'This is their first quit attempt.', once/a_few/many/countless with natural language
NULL previousAttempts omits quit history line from prompt
Custom triggers (custom: prefix) have prefix stripped and display naturally alongside predefined trigger display names
Double-prefixed triggers (custom:custom:) have only first prefix stripped
User-provided text (preferred_name, custom trigger text) wrapped in triple-backtick delimiters in the USER CONTEXT block
sanitizeForPrompt() imported and applied to user-provided text
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File: server/utils/personalization/context-builder.ts. Add QUIT_ATTEMPTS_CONTEXT map and formatTriggers() helper. Import sanitizeForPrompt from sanitize-user-text.ts. See spec for TRIGGER_DISPLAY_NAMES mapping.

userStories.title: Create PreferredNameStep component
userStories.description: As a user starting onboarding, I want to be asked for my preferred name in a friendly, optional step, so that my AI coach can address me personally.
acceptanceCriteria: New file created at components/intake/PreferredNameStep.vue using <script setup lang='ts'>
Heading text: 'What should we call you?'
Helper text: 'So your coach can address you personally'
Text input with maxlength='50', placeholder 'Your first name or nickname', glass-input rounded-pill styling
Next button is always enabled regardless of input state
No Back button on this step
Component uses v-model pattern: props modelValue (string), emits update:modelValue and next
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Follow the same pattern as existing step components (e.g., ProductTypeStep.vue). Use glass-input, rounded-pill, border-brand-border styling consistent with other inputs in the app.

userStories.title: Update QuitHistoryStep for vertical stack and string values
userStories.description: As a user on the quit history step, I want to select from descriptive labels in a vertical layout, so that I can more accurately describe my quit history.
acceptanceCriteria: previousAttempts prop type changed from number | undefined to string | undefined
emit update:previousAttempts emits string | undefined (was number | undefined)

attemptOptions replaced with 5 options: {value:'never',label:'Never'}, {value:'once',label:'Once'}, {value:'a_few',label:'A few times'}, {value:'many',label:'Many times'}, {value:'countless',label:'Too many to count'}
Layout changed from grid grid-cols-4 gap-3 to flex flex-col gap-3 (vertical full-width buttons)
Buttons use same border-2 rounded-xl styling as before
Longest quit duration conditional changed from previousAttempts > 0 to previousAttempts && previousAttempts !== 'never'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File: components/intake/QuitHistoryStep.vue. This is now Step 4 (was Step 3) due to the new preferred name Step 1, but the component itself doesn't need to know its step number.

userStories.title: Update TriggersStep with Other option and free-text input
userStories.description: As a user with a unique trigger, I want to describe my custom trigger when selecting 'Other', so that my AI coach knows about my specific triggers.
acceptanceCriteria: Other option added as last item in options array with value 'other', label 'Other'
Other button spans full width using col-span-2 class
When Other is selected, text input slides down below grid with CSS transition (v-show + max-height/opacity/ease-out)
Text input has maxlength='100', placeholder 'Describe your trigger...', glass-input styling
Text input container has aria-live='polite' for screen reader announcement
Text input auto-focused when Other is selected via nextTick
Start My Journey button disabled when Other is selected and text input is empty
Hint text 'Please describe your trigger or deselect Other.' shown when submit is disabled
Deselecting Other hides input but preserves typed text in component state (local ref)
Re-selecting Other restores previously typed text
On submit: 'other' in triggers array replaced with 'custom:{trimmed text}'
If user text starts with 'custom:', stored as 'custom:custom:{text}' (double-prefix escape)
Whitespace-only custom text treated as empty (submit stays disabled)
On mount with existing triggers containing custom: entry: Other is selected, text extracted from prefix
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File: components/intake/TriggersStep.vue. Local state: customTriggerText ref (preserved across toggle), otherTextInput template ref for auto-focus. CSS transition uses v-show with max-height + opacity + ease-out to match app animation style.

userStories.title: Update IntakeForm for 6 steps and preferred name integration
userStories.description: As a user in the onboarding flow, I want the form to have 6 steps with preferred name as Step 1, so that I can provide my name before product/usage questions.
acceptanceCriteria: Progress indicator shows 6 dots (changed from v-for step in 5 to v-for step in 6)
Step 1 renders IntakePreferredNameStep with v-model for formData.preferredName
Steps 2-6 render existing components (ProductType, UsageFrequency, QuitHistory, PrimaryReason, Triggers) shifted by +1
Back from Step 2 returns to Step 1, back from Step 1 does nothing
formData reactive object includes preferredName: string field (default '')
New props accepted: prefilledName?: string, existingIntake?: IntakeResponse | null
On mount: if existingIntake provided, populate all formData fields from it (including preferred_name mapped to preferredName, previous_attempts mapped to previousAttempts as string)
On mount: if prefilledName provided and no existing preferred_name, set formData.preferredName = prefilledName
existingIntake.preferred_name takes priority over prefilledName prop
handleSubmit includes preferredName in the saveIntake payload
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File: components/intake/IntakeForm.vue. Import IntakeResponse type from useIntake composable. The re-entry pre-fill maps snake_case DB fields to camelCase formData fields.

userStories.title: Update onboarding page for background data fetching
userStories.description: As a returning user, I want my previous data pre-filled, and as a Google SSO user, I want my first name suggested, so I don't have to re-enter information.
acceptanceCriteria: Welcome screen renders immediately on page load (no loading delay)
On mount: parallel background fetch via Promise.allSettled() for profile and existing intake
Profile fetch extracts first name from profiles.full_name (first word before space) into prefilledName ref
If profile fetch fails, prefilledName stays empty (no error shown, per FR-5.6)
If intake fetch succeeds with data, existingIntake ref is populated
If intake fetch fails, existingIntake stays null (no error shown)
prefilledName and existingIntake passed as props to IntakeForm
If user taps Let's Go while fetches still in progress, brief loading state shown before form renders
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File: pages/onboarding.vue. Use useAuth().getProfile() for profile fetch and useIntake().fetchIntake() for intake data. Add isDataLoading ref to track loading state.

userStories.title: Unit tests for sanitization utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I want comprehensive unit tests for the prompt sanitization function, so that I can be confident user text is safely sanitized.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file created at tests/unit/utils/sanitize-user-text.test.ts using Vitest
Tests: strips SYSTEM/ASSISTANT/Human/AI/User role markers (case-insensitive)
Tests: strips markdown headers (### etc)
Tests: strips triple backticks
Tests: strips HTML/XML tags
Tests: preserves normal names ('Kevin', 'Skip', 'Ignore')
Tests: preserves unicode ('José') and emoji
Tests: trims whitespace, handles empty string
Tests: handles multiline injection attempts
Tests pass
userStories.notes: See spec v1.1: Test Specification section for the full test case table with inputs and expected outputs.

userStories.title: Unit tests for context builder v1.1 changes
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests verifying the context builder correctly formats preferred name, quit attempts, and custom triggers for prompt injection.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file created at tests/unit/utils/context-builder.test.ts using Vitest
Tests: preferred name included in prompt when present, omitted when null or empty
Tests: quit attempts mapped to natural language for all 5 values (never/once/a_few/many/countless)
Tests: NULL previousAttempts omits quit history line
Tests: custom triggers have custom: prefix stripped and display naturally
Tests: double-prefixed triggers (custom:custom:) have only first prefix stripped
Tests: user-provided text wrapped in triple-backtick delimiters
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Test formatContextForPrompt() directly by passing pre-built PersonalizationContext objects. No Supabase mock needed for formatter tests.

userStories.title: Update existing E2E onboarding tests for 6-step flow
userStories.description: As a developer, I want existing E2E tests updated so they pass with the new 6-step onboarding flow.
acceptanceCriteria: All step count assertions updated from 5 to 6
Progress indicator expectations updated to 6 dots
Step navigation assertions account for new Step 1 (preferred name)
Mock data includes preferred_name and string previous_attempts where needed
Import { test, expect } from './fixtures' (never from @playwright/test)
All existing onboarding E2E tests pass
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Files: tests/e2e/onboarding.spec.ts and any other test files referencing onboarding steps. Review each test to ensure it navigates through Step 1 (preferred name) before reaching the steps it actually tests.

userStories.title: New E2E tests for v1.1 onboarding enhancements
userStories.description: As a developer, I want E2E tests covering preferred name, quit attempts vertical stack, and custom triggers to verify all three enhancements work correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: new user can enter a name on Step 1 and proceed to Step 2
Test: new user can skip name (leave empty) and proceed
Test: 5 vertically stacked quit attempt buttons visible with correct labels (Never, Once, A few times, Many times, Too many to count)
Test: selecting Never hides longest quit duration question
Test: selecting any other quit option shows longest quit duration question
Test: selecting Other trigger reveals text input that is auto-focused
Test: Start My Journey disabled when Other selected with empty input
Test: typing custom trigger and submitting includes custom: prefix in payload
Test: deselecting Other hides input, re-selecting restores text
Import { test, expect } from './fixtures' (never from @playwright/test)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: File: tests/e2e/onboarding.spec.ts. Use page.route() to mock API calls and verify submitted payloads. Use existing mock utilities from tests/e2e/utils/.
